data.title: Fed's Balance Sheet Reduction Could Impact Markets, Analyst Suggests
data.description:
Wilson Asset Management's portfolio strategist, Damian Boey, has commented on the potential implications of a Federal Reserve policy shift. According to PANews, Boey noted that Walsh has expressed a preference for lowering interest rates. However, the condition for achieving this is the reduction of the Federal Reserve's balance sheet.

The market is contemplating the potential consequences of a shrinking balance sheet. If the Federal Reserve begins to withdraw foundational support, assets traditionally used to hedge against balance sheet expansion, such as gold, cryptocurrencies, and bonds, may face sell-offs.

data.title: Bitcoin Futures Liquidation Dominance Oscillator Shows High Long Position Liquidations
data.description: Bitcoin futures liquidation dominance oscillator indicates that long position liquidations reached 97% yesterday. According to Odaily, the 30-day moving average has risen to 31.4%, suggesting that nearly all forced liquidations in the market are from long positions, with buyers facing systemic pressure over the past month. Despite price declines and consecutive liquidations, the perpetual contract funding rate for Bitcoin remains positive, with an annualized rate of 43.2% yesterday. Although this figure is below the peak of over 100% seen from October to November, it still demonstrates a strong demand for long positions in the market. The maintenance of a positive funding rate amid large-scale liquidations increases the risk of further deleveraging, indicating that long positions are recovering quickly or have not been fully liquidated. Currently, the funding rate has not shifted to a neutral or negative zone, reflecting that the derivatives market has not completed a thorough liquidation.

data.title: Institutions: The market views Warsh as a "moderate hawk" and a guardian of independence.
data.description: Kristina Clifton, senior economist and senior currency strategist at the Commonwealth Bank of Australia, said the dollar did indeed seem to rise on the news, which she believes is because the market generally perceives Warsh to be slightly less dovish than another candidate, Hassett. Therefore, today's market volatility is merely a minor fluctuation based on this, and Warsh may be better positioned to uphold the Fed's independence than some other candidates. "We haven't heard much from him yet, but he has made some comments that essentially express a desire for strict adherence to the Fed's responsibility to control inflation. He has also served as a Fed governor and does not support quantitative easing, so this may again indicate that he is slightly more hawkish than some other potential candidates." (Jinshi)

data.title: aelf will allocate an additional 2 million ELF to eBridge to enhance cross-chain liquidity.
data.description: AI-enhanced Layer 1 blockchain aelf has officially announced an additional allocation of 2,000,000 ELF tokens to the eBridge cross-chain bridge to further improve cross-chain liquidity and asset transfer efficiency. Previously, aelf successfully allocated 5,000,000 ELF tokens to eBridge to support cross-chain transfers of ELF tokens between the aelf mainnet and the Ethereum network. It is understood that these transfers will continue to be executed through the official aelf management address to ensure transparency. Specific transaction information and on-chain confirmation details will be disclosed in subsequent announcements after the transfers are completed.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin long positions were liquidated at a rate of 96.7%, and funding rates remained positive, indicating that the market has not yet completed deleveraging.
data.description: Crypto market analyst Axel Adler Jr. writes that the Bitcoin futures liquidation dominance oscillator shows that long positions accounted for 97% of liquidations yesterday, with the 30-day moving average rising to 31.4%. This figure means that almost all forced liquidations in the market came from long positions, indicating that buyers have faced continuous systemic pressure over the past month. Despite the price decline and the chain of liquidations, the funding rate for Bitcoin perpetual contracts remained positive, with an annualized rate of 43.2% yesterday. While this indicator is lower than the peak of over 100% during October and November, it still suggests that demand for long positions dominates the market. The positive funding rate during a large-scale liquidation increases the risk of further deleveraging, indicating that long positions in the market have either recovered rapidly or have not yet been fully liquidated. The fact that the funding rate has not yet moved into neutral or negative territory reflects that the derivatives market has not yet completed a thorough liquidation. (axeladlerjr)

data.title: A whale received 445,000 HYPE tokens from Galaxy Digital OTC and may pledge them.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale has received 445,000 HYPE tokens from Galaxy Digital OTC, which it may stake, worth approximately $14.48 million. To date, this whale has received 1.355 million HYPE tokens ($39.72 million) from Galaxy Digital OTC, of ​​which 465,000 HYPE tokens ($13.5 million) have already been staked.

data.title: Strategists warn that Warsh's "rate cut declaration" may contain hidden traps, the cost of which could be a global liquidity crunch.
data.description: Damian Boyle, portfolio strategist at Wilson Asset Management, said that Warsh has publicly expressed a preference for lower interest rates. However, his trade-off for rate cuts was the Federal Reserve's desire to shrink its balance sheet. The market's reaction seems to be a question: what would the world be like if the Fed's balance sheet shrank? Once the talk of removing the foundational support begins, all assets that people have been using to hedge against balance sheet expansion, such as gold, cryptocurrencies, and obviously bonds, will begin to be sold off. (Jinshi)

data.title: Nomura Plans Stock Buyback Despite Profit Decline
data.description:
Nomura has announced a plan to repurchase its shares, even as its third-quarter profits have decreased. According to Jin10, Japan's largest securities firm is still on track to achieve a record annual profit. In a statement released on Friday, the company reported a 9.7% drop in net profit for the fiscal third quarter ending December 31, amounting to 91.6 billion yen (approximately $5.95 billion). This result fell short of the average forecast of 95.1 billion yen by four analysts.

CEO Kentaro Okuda is aiming to maintain the momentum of last year's record profits, as financial institutions are currently striving to capitalize on the volatility and investment surge in the Japanese market. With increasing attention on Japan's fiscal policy direction, securities firms are playing a crucial role in assisting clients to navigate the fluctuating bond and currency markets. Nomura's net profit for the first nine months totaled 288.2 billion yen, positioning the company to surpass the previous fiscal year's record high of 340.7 billion yen.

data.title: Gold, Silver, and Copper Prices Plummet, Triggering $120 Million Token Sell-Off
data.description: Prices of gold, silver, and copper have experienced a significant decline, leading to a $120 million sell-off in blockchain tokens that replicate these metals. According to NS3.AI, despite the downturn in metal prices, Bitcoin remains unaffected, trading independently and underscoring its growing role as a standalone risk asset. This divergence indicates a shift in investor behavior, with cryptocurrencies increasingly being viewed separately from traditional commodities.

data.title: a16z co-founders followed Moltbook's official Twitter account, and Moltbook briefly surpassed $25 million, setting a new record.
data.description: The recent surge in MOLT's market capitalization may have been triggered by Marc Andreessen's X account following MLTbook with leverage. According to GMGN data, MOLT's market capitalization briefly surpassed $25 million, reaching a new all-time high, and is currently trading at $19.77 million, a 1-hour increase of 107%. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coin is highly volatile, and investors should participate with caution.

data.title: Bitcoin’s Value vs Gold Nears 2017 Levels Despite “Hype,” Peter Schiff Says
data.description:
Bitcoin’s value against gold has fallen near 2017 levels, reviving doubts about its role as a long-term store of value.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/bitcoins-value-vs-gold-nears-2017-levels-despite-hype-peter-schiff-says/

data.title: Citi India Appoints Ankur Khurana as Head of Commercial Banking
data.description:
Citi's India division has appointed Ankur Khurana as the head of its commercial banking unit. According to Jin10, the Wall Street bank aims to enhance its services for India's rapidly expanding mid-sized enterprises. Khurana brings over two decades of banking experience, having previously served as the co-head of coverage for India and South Asia at Standard Chartered Bank. The position had been vacant since July of the previous year when veteran banker Bhanu Vohra left to join the Indian branch of global consultancy BDO. 

India is witnessing a surge in transaction activities, with significant growth in equity capital markets and mergers and acquisitions. As Indian companies continue to expand and engage in overseas acquisitions, the mid-market sector presents extensive opportunities.

data.title: MGBX will list clawd.atg.eth (CLAWD) spot trading.
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will be listed on clawd.atg.eth (CLAWD) on January 30, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Spot trading deposit opening time: January 30, 2026 at 17:00 (SGT). Trading opening time: January 30, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Withdrawal opening time: February 2, 2026 at 19:00 (SGT). The clawd.atg.eth project is a very popular AI Agent + Meme coin experiment on the Base chain, initiated and deeply involved by Austin Griffith, a well-known developer of the Ethereum Foundation.

data.title: Upbit releases audit report on digital assets and deposits: 105.72% of its funds are available for customer payments.
data.description: Upbit released an "Audit Report on Digital Assets and Deposits" audited by Sunghyun Certified Public Accountants on January 1, 2026, which disclosed that Upbit holds approximately 103.19% of the digital assets it intends to pay its customers, and the deposit audit results show that Upbit holds 105.72% of the funds available for paying its customers.

data.title: Nikita Bier, product manager for X, initiated a poll regarding "closing down the X community".
data.description: Nikita Bier, X Product Manager and Solana Consultant, launched a poll asking, "Would you be happy or sad if X Community were shut down?" The poll will end in 23 hours, and currently, 53.9% are sad and 46.1% are happy.

data.title: Trader Engages in High-Leverage Short Position on Ethereum
data.description:
On January 30, a trader executed a short position on 601.94 ETH using 25x leverage at an average entry price of $2730.93. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the position is currently showing a slight unrealized profit.

The trader is known for favoring high-leverage short strategies and has recently demonstrated strong performance with frequent large-scale opening and closing of positions.

data.title: CZ: FUD Does Not Hurt the Target but Damages the Market
data.description: Changpeng Zhao (CZ), Binance founder and former CEO, posted on X stating that FUD (fear, uncertainty, and doubt) does not hurt targets such as himself, but it negatively impacts the broader market.CZ stated that neither he nor Binance has engaged in any meaningful large-scale selling. He explained that his personal selling is limited to routine card payments, giving an example where approximately $5 worth of BNB is converted to cover everyday expenses such as coffee.He added that while he no longer runs Binance, based on his understanding, the company only converts a portion of its revenue to cover operating expenses and remains a net holder overall. CZ also noted that Binance now has a global regulator that is able to review trades across accounts on the platform.CZ emphasized that the public should avoid being misinformed and encouraged individuals to focus their efforts on positive self-improvement.

data.title: Potential Hawkish Shift in Federal Reserve Leadership Raises Market Concerns
data.description: On January 30, Jin10 reported that Sonu Varghese, a global macro strategist at Carson Group, expressed concerns about the potential nomination of Kevin Warsh as the Federal Reserve Chair. According to Jin10, Varghese noted that Warsh has historically been hawkish, despite his recent discussions on interest rate cuts. If Warsh advocates for significant rate reductions upon joining the Federal Reserve, he may struggle to convince others of the necessity for further cuts. This scenario could lead to a deeply divided committee that refrains from implementing rate cuts altogether. In the short term, a potentially hawkish Federal Reserve could increase market volatility.
